steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage ends by stating that understanding how to prevent horizontal gene transfer 'might result in the mitigation of dangerous pathogens.' The question asks for the passage's main idea, so the correct choice must capture the passage's emphasis on horizontal gene transfer's potential real-world applications.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — A common perception of horizontal gene transfer is inaccurate.: describes a common perception, but the passage never mentions one. ✗
  • Choice B — Horizontal gene transfer is more common than was initially thought.: claims horizontal gene transfer is 'more common' than some alternative — no such comparison appears in the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — The results of new research into horizontal gene transfer cast doubt on earlier findings.: says new research casts doubt on earlier findings, but the passage never mentions earlier findings being challenged. ✗
  • Choice D — The study of horizontal gene transfer may yield findings with useful applications.: directly reflects the passage's conclusion about mitigating dangerous pathogens. ✓
That matches Choice D — The study of horizontal gene transfer may yield findings with useful applications..
